preview_text:
The model favours Germany to win at 35.5%, but Ecuador poses a credible threat with a 32.9% chance of victory. The gap separating the two teams is modest, suggesting competitive conditions rather than a clear hierarchy. Germany's edge rests on a narrow margin of less than 3 percentage points in the model's assessment.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 31.5%, reinforcing a tightly matched contest. All three results occupy a similar probability band, with no single outcome commanding overwhelming likelihood. This spread indicates a fixture unlikely to be decided by dominance but rather by fine margins or decisive moments.

The market diverges materially from the model, assigning Germany a substantially higher win probability of 55.8% and Ecuador only 19.3%—a gap of roughly 20 percentage points in Germany's favour. Both sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices align closely with each other but depart significantly from the statistical forecast. The dashboard presents both signals without adjudicating their accuracy; users should weigh the discrepancy when forming their own assessments.
